# Questions in Livestream 212

Live Stream #212: One Degree of Separation

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=841" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:01</a> | Which version of Reliquary of the Dead is best to get in order to support you? | He says the softcover supports him best financially, while the hardcover has extra artwork. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=1387" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:07</a> | Is there any record of Yahweh before the Pentateuch outside the Bible, perhaps under another name? | He says no secure pre-Pentateuch attestation is known; proposed earlier references are too ambiguous to be persuasive. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=1695" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:15</a> | What is a biblical response to overrealized demonology? | He argues believers can be influenced but not possessed, and he rejects blaming ordinary sin or conflict on demons. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=2185" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">36:25</a> | Does God condemn any specific economic system? | He says Scripture gives moral principles rather than a full system, but some systems fit those principles far worse than others. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=2713" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:13</a> | How would you respond to the claim that Scandinavian welfare states are a successful rebuttal to criticism of communism? | He says Scandinavian countries are capitalist welfare states, not communist or socialist examples. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=2870" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:50</a> | Why do you think Nimrod is an amalgamation, and of whom? | He treats Nimrod as a composite memory of Mesopotamian kings, but says the exact figures behind it remain uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=2978" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:38</a> | Do you agree with the reading of Proverbs 22:6 as letting a child have his own way? | No. He argues that reading does not fit the Hebrew and keeps the sense closer to training or instruction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=3445" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:25</a> | Did the ancient Egyptians ever need to go to war with ancient Sudan over stopping the Nile? | He says no; ancient states did not have the engineering capacity to shut down the Nile that way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=3606" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:06</a> | Have Moabite palaces and fortresses been excavated? | Yes. He says major Moabite sites have been excavated, even if the full corpus is not easy to summarize quickly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=3743" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:23</a> | Wouldn't a dam only delay the flow for a while? | He says a dam regulates release through stored water and floodgates, so it can do more than create a momentary delay. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=4000" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:40</a> | Is Judges 1:27 a problem for a 19th-dynasty conquest model? | He says no; leftover garrison centers could persist after a broader conquest and then decline later. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=4491" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:51</a> | Could the Egyptian material culture in question just be elite emulation? | He says elite emulation alone does not explain the broader pattern of ordinary material culture and administration. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=4571" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:16:11</a> | Is Dallas Theological Seminary a good school? | He gives a mixed answer: strong in languages and some cognate work, weaker in other areas. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=4713" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:33</a> | What inspired you to pursue an MD? | He says pastoral interests and a shift away from Unix and systems work pushed him toward ministry training. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=5032" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:52</a> | Don't all pastors need a degree in used car sales nowadays? | He says the joke is uncomfortably close to his experience reviewing pastoral resumes, including many with weak or false credentials. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=5449" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:49</a> | Should pastors use AI to write their sermons? | He rejects that strongly and treats AI sermon-writing as a bad substitute for real study and exegesis. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=6023" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:23</a> | Do you have a favorite novel? | Yes. He names Dune as his favorite novel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=6048" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:48</a> | What books do you recommend on the spirit world? | He points viewers to the bibliography he already posted on his community page rather than rebuilding the list live. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=6264" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:44:24</a> | What does Selah mean in Psalms? | He says the exact sense is uncertain and common suggestions remain educated guesses. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=6298" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:44:58</a> | What time of day did people typically bathe in the ancient Near East? | He says ritual purpose mattered more than clock time, so practice varied with context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=6469" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47:49</a> | Did they practice pouring rather than full immersion? | Generally yes in the bathing context under discussion; he describes pouring as the normal pattern. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=6564" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:24</a> | Did Christians copy Egyptian practices? | He says ritual practices shared a common ancient Near Eastern language, so similarities are not simple plagiarism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=6988" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:56:28</a> | What do you think of Kagemusha? | He says it is good, though not among his very top films. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=7125" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:58:45</a> | What do you think of the recent Myth Vision podcast videos? | He is cautious and says he is still judging the situation rather than giving a firm verdict. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=7187" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:59:47</a> | Why wouldn't I fit into Moody? | He jokes that Moody's legalistic culture would be restrictive and says even appearance standards would be an issue. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=7461" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:04:21</a> | Was he part of the crowd that got your library privileges revoked? | He says yes, and says the consequences went beyond library privileges to lost academic and publication opportunities. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=7621" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:07:01</a> | Does that sound like lawsuit-level stuff? | He says they considered it, but jurisdiction and cost made a lawsuit impractical. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=7800" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:00</a> | Who proved it was slander? | He credits Pine Creek with checking the full context and challenging the accusation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=7916" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:11:56</a> | Didn't your pastor also do a video? | He says yes, but credits Pine Creek with spearheading the public vindication. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=8151" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:51</a> | How has it been finding academic work in the two years since you were both fired? | He says it was effectively impossible at first, though conditions have improved somewhat more recently. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=8321" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:41</a> | Since going full-time for a while now, have things been stable for you all? | He says it has been up and down financially, though they are continuing the channel's work and seeing some hopeful changes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=8451" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:20:51</a> | Do you have any academic opportunities anymore? | He says no formal opportunities remain, even though the books still circulate in academic settings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=8909" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:29</a> | Wasn't Reuben disqualified as firstborn? | He says Reuben was effectively displaced rather than formally stripped in a simple legal sense. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=8972" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:32</a> | What did Ruth do to Boaz in Ruth 3? | He treats it as an intimate but honorable marriage overture, not as a crude or pornographic scene. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=9588" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:39:48</a> | Could a good drama film be made from Ruth? | Possibly yes, though he doubts it would become a mass-market hit. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=9763" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:43</a> | Why did the Semites live in the temple district at Avaris? | He says the district was a religious center with Semitic temples, so it made sense for them to cluster there. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=9812" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:43:32</a> | Can you elaborate on law versus order in the ancient Near East? | He contrasts Mesopotamian law-heavy framing with Egypt's stronger emphasis on social order and tradition. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=10046" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:47:26</a> | If Noah and his wife had adult time, how would Noah know the father of Canaan? | He answers in straightforward biological terms: pregnancy would eventually reveal paternity within the story world. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=10154" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:14</a> | What is your opinion of constructed languages such as Esperanto? | He says they can be fun and useful, but natural languages will always dominate actual long-term use. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=10657" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:57:37</a> | Why did the devil create calculus? | He jokes that calculus is evil, then says the heavy memorization of formulas was what made it difficult for him. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=10985" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:05</a> | Is the KTU 1 phrase claimed as "son of El/Yahweh" valid, and does Ugaritic ignore silent he letters? | He is not convinced; he says Ugaritic does not ignore silent he letters and the proposed reading sounds stretched. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=11204" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:06:44</a> | What are good topics of discussion for a young Christian adults group? | He recommends taking them deep into the Bible and helping them own the faith for themselves. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=11744" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:15:44</a> | Is there evidence of YouTube shenanigans behind the channel's slower growth? | Yes. He thinks algorithmic distribution effects are real, though also fairly normal for the platform. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=11864" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:17:44</a> | Have you gotten help from Inspiring Philosophy or other larger Christian channels? | He says he has a friendly relationship with Inspiring Philosophy, but not broad backing from larger channels. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=12141" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:22:21</a> | What is your best advice for someone entering grad school and aiming for academia? | He advises realism about institutional politics and says survival often depends on keeping your head down. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=12594" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:54</a> | How did Kenneth Kitchen get through academic politics like that? | He says Kitchen largely bulldozed through it by being indifferent to collegial games and incentives. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=12716" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:31:56</a> | What about the treaty parallels Kitchen argued for? | He says Kitchen took criticism there too because those arguments cut against dominant consensus positions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=12980" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:36:20</a> | Don't controversial books sell well? | He says controversy alone does not sell books; sales depend more on channels, publicity, writing, and presentation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=13195" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:39:55</a> | What is your favorite dinosaur, and why? | He names a small feathered favorite first, then lands more broadly on spinosaur-type dinosaurs as especially appealing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=13267" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:41:07</a> | What is the difference between a pastor and a priest, and did pastor come from the Reformation? | He says pastor means shepherding role, priest implies a different sacramental function, and pastor is not merely a Reformation invention. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=13602" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:46:42</a> | Is the two-powers-in-heaven idea a valid Old Testament view? | He says it has some validity as an interpretive pattern, though he treats it carefully rather than absolutely. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=14189" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:56:29</a> | Did the ancient Egyptians think Osiris was a real figure in the shape of an animal? | He says Osiris was normally human-formed, with animal associations mainly through composite or incarnated forms. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=14235" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:57:15</a> | Was reusing Avaris as a graveyard a sign of contempt for the city? | He says no; it is better read as practical reuse of abandoned space than symbolic hatred. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/4H4OC1XPI-U?t=14918" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:08:38</a> | Is tracing the origins of Yahweh worship a waste of time? | He says it can be worthwhile, but present evidence sets hard limits on how far the inquiry can go. |
